_comment: Convergence-PERFORMANCE budgets for js/test/perf.spec.ts (run via `npm run perf`). Keyed [task][profile]. Batches-to-converge is engine-stable, so budgets are per-PROFILE, not per-device; these are REGRESSION GUARDS at the current status quo, not the 30s product budget — a ceiling well below the 800-batch fallback so a collapse or a real regression fails, but with headroom over the observed slow seeds so healthy runs don't flake. `maxBatchesToConverge` is the hard gate and the fail-fast ceiling; `requireConvergedByLoss` asserts the run crossed the loss threshold rather than hitting the max-batch fallback; `minGraspRate` (0 disables) is a soft closed-loop quality floor set well below the observed worst seed (grasp is noisy over a handful of episodes — this catches a break, it does not police quality). MEASUREMENT BASIS (2026-07, current carry-flag pick-up arch, converge.loss 0.015): desktop 8c/4b converged by loss at 415/423/440/506/618 batches (grasp 58-96%); mobile 4c/3b at 283/286/326/404 batches (grasp 37-88%); 0/9 hit the fallback. Add a task = add a sibling key here + a ?task= hook in js/test/harness/main.ts.
